description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a cylindrical shape, slightly bulging at the center and narrowing towards the top and bottom. The body is constructed from dark, vertically aligned wooden staves, held together by three wide, weathered metal hoops—two near the top and bottom, and one at the midsection. The top surface is closed with a wooden lid, which is flush with the rim and features a small, round wooden plug or bung on one side. The wood grain and subtle wear on the metal bands suggest it has been exposed to the elements, giving it a rustic, utilitarian appearance. There are no visible handles or decorative markings, emphasizing its functional design. This type of barrel is commonly found in urban settings such as alleys, storage yards, or near workshops, used for storage or as a receptacle.
description: A weathered wooden barrel with dark staves and three metal hoops, topped with a flush wooden lid and a small plug. Its rustic, utilitarian look suggests use for storage in urban environments.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wooden staves, three metal hoops, and the lid with a visible plug and metal fasteners on the upper hoop.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the continuous wood grain and metal hoops, with the plug less visible and no fasteners apparent.
description_view_2: Angled view where the plug on the lid is visible, and the metal hoops appear evenly spaced with no fasteners shown.
description_view_3: Rear view with the plug on the lid visible but no fasteners, showing the smooth curvature of the barrel and the uniformity of the hoops.
